metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete parking mechanism for N=5 re-drive exhaustion (e.g. 'POST a Gerrit review comment with message X and remove the Autosubmit+1 vote by setting Autosubmit=0 via the bot identity') so the executor does not need to design the interface.
findings.finding: 'Park the change and surface needs_rebase' after N=5 re-drives is undefined: no mechanism is specified (label vote? Gerrit message? status field?), so an executor cannot implement it without inventing the interface.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'On N=5 exhaustion: post a Gerrit review comment "Auto-land failed after 5 rebase attempts; manual rebase required" and set Autosubmit=0 (clearing the queue label) via the bot service account; set wipChain.phase = idle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Scope section with AC4: either explicitly carve the N=5 re-drive cap and park/`needs_rebase` path INTO this story's scope, or move them to the named failure-paths story and add a placeholder AC referencing that story.
findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly excludes failure paths ('Failure paths, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories'), yet AC4 mandates a detailed failure/retry path — a bounded re-drive loop (N=5), a parking action, and surfacing 'needs_rebase' — all of which are failure-path behavior. This creates a cross-section contradiction: an implementer reading Scope would defer these behaviors to a future story, while the ACs require them here.
findings.location: Scope section vs AC4 ('re-drive the same `wipChain`... looping at most N=5 times; after N=5 re-drives park the change and surface `needs_rebase`')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to Scope: 'Exception: the TOCTOU re-drive exit condition (N=5 cap → park → `needs_rebase`) is in-scope here as it is integral to the safe-submit invariant.' This disambiguates without expanding scope unexpectedly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify how the bot handles multi-member chains in `awaiting_verified`: specify whether `tested_sha` becomes a per-member map (`chain_member_ids[] → sha`), or the bot polls `is:submittable` (which enforces Verified=MAX per-change via submit requirements) to confirm ALL members have a fresh Verified on their post-rebase patchset SHA before submitting.
findings.finding: For a multi-member relation chain, POST /rebase:chain mints a new patchset for EVERY chain member — each with its own distinct patchset SHA. The wipChain schema stores a single `tested_sha`, and AC3's 'fresh Verified +1 on the exact rebased patchset SHA' check is written as a singular check. The plan does not specify how the bot verifies that ALL chain members have a fresh Verified +1 on their respective post-rebase patchset SHAs before submitting. Submitting while one intermediate member lacks a fresh Verified would land an untested tree on main, violating the core R5 safety guarantee.
findings.location: AC3 ('Awaits a fresh Verified +1'), AC5 ('wipChain schema: ... tested_sha ... set to the post-rebase patchset SHA')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the singular `tested_sha` field with a `member_shas: dict[change_id → post_rebase_sha]` populated after rebase:chain completes (by reading each member's `current_revision` from GET /changes/{id}). In awaiting_verified, poll until ALL member_shas have a Verified+1 on their respective SHA. This also makes the TOCTOU guard precise: re-drive if ANY member's current_revision no longer matches its recorded SHA.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `POST /rebase:chain` with the concrete Gerrit REST form: `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with body `{"rebase_chain": true}` (the `RebaseInput.rebase_chain` field), and add a Gerrit 3.14.1 docs/changelog citation or a test call confirming this parameter is supported on the installed version.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` as a Gerrit REST endpoint. This path form does not exist in Gerrit's REST API — chain rebase is `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with a `RebaseInput` JSON body containing `{"rebase_chain": true}`. The colon-action sub-resource form (e.g. `/submit`, `/abandon`) is used in Gerrit REST but `/rebase:chain` is not a documented endpoint in any Gerrit 3.x release. A prior plan-review pass also flagged this (cache file e51094f3860153b05e50.json). Implementing against a non-existent path causes a runtime 404, silently failing the chain rebase.
findings.location: AC — Rebase-selection rule: 'chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with `POST /rebase:chain`'
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC rebase-selection rule, replace '`POST /rebase:chain`' with '`POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `{"rebase_chain": true}`'. Add a note: 'Verified against Gerrit 3.14.1: `RebaseInput.rebase_chain` field supported since Gerrit 3.x (REST API reference: changes.html#rebase-change).'  This mirrors the existing pattern of citing 3.14.1 quirks explicitly (e.g. `NoBlock`, g2p compact-id fix).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `re_drive_count` (or equivalent) to the `wipChain` schema in AC5 so the N=5 re-drive counter referenced in AC4 has an unambiguous, phase-reset-safe location.
findings.finding: The N=5 re-drive counter is referenced in the AC4 decision rule ('looping at most N=5 times') but has no corresponding field in the wipChain schema defined in AC5. The schema lists {change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase} — no re_drive_count or equivalent. An implementor cannot determine where this counter lives (in-memory local variable, wipChain field, or separate map) and whether it survives a phase reset.
findings.location: AC4 (TOCTOU guard + re-drive) and AC5 (wipChain schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC5 schema to: `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase, re_drive_count}` where `re_drive_count` increments on each `rebasing` re-entry triggered by a TOCTOU failure and is reset to 0 only when `wipChain` is cleared (on `idle`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'park' mechanism for the post-N=5 fallback in AC4: specify a Gerrit-side action (e.g. post a review message, set a label, or abandon), add a terminal phase value (e.g. `needs_rebase`) to the AC5 phase enum, and clarify what wipChain state persists for S4/S5 to observe.
findings.finding: The terminal failure action 'park the change and surface needs_rebase' (triggered after N=5 re-drives) is specified as a fallback outcome in AC4, but neither the mechanism nor the phase state is defined anywhere in the plan. The phase vocabulary in AC5 is {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle} — there is no 'parked', 'failed', or 'needs_rebase' phase value. No Gerrit-side action is specified (no label vote, no message, no review comment), leaving the fallback entirely unimplementable.
findings.location: AC4 (re-drive + park fallback) and AC5 (wipChain schema / phase enum)

findings.finding: The singular `tested_sha` field in the wipChain schema cannot represent all post-rebase patchset SHAs for a multi-member relation chain, leaving N-1 chain members' SHA integrity unchecked in the TOCTOU guard before submit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema AC and TOCTOU guard AC
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the scalar `tested_sha` with a map `tested_shas: dict[change_id, sha]` populated for all `chain_member_ids` after `/rebase:chain` completes. The TOCTOU pre-submit check must assert `currentRevision == tested_shas[id]` for every member; if any member drifted, trigger the re-drive loop.

findings.finding: 'Park the change and surface needs_rebase' is not a specific observable outcome — 'park' and 'surface' are effort-framed verbs with no defined system-state change, making the criterion non-measurable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard AC (N=5 exhaustion clause)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete proving command per acceptance criterion (e.g. a pytest invocation against a mock Gerrit fixture for the happy-path loop, and a specific curl sequence against a Gerrit sandbox for the FFO guard) so done-ness is verifiable without executor design work.
findings.finding: No proving command or test strategy is described for any of the seven acceptance criteria; an executor has no way to verify the loop is correct without designing the test harness from scratch.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all seven ACs)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing section (or explicit per-AC verification steps) covering: (a) unit tests for the state-machine transitions and re-drive counter boundary (N=5); (b) unit tests for the merge-vs-non-merge rebase-selection rule; (c) a live/end-to-end smoke-test AC exercising the full rebase → await Verified → FFO-check → submit path against a real or staging Gerrit instance, so the happy path is verified as it will run in production.
findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new conditional logic (phase state machine with 5 states, re-drive loop up to N=5, FFO TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering, merge-vs-non-merge rebase-selection branching) but contains zero testing specification — no unit tests, integration tests, or live/end-to-end acceptance criteria are mentioned, and no child ticket or deferral rationale is cited.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria and Scope sections

findings.finding: No proving command exists for any acceptance criterion — the plan contains zero test strategy, CI check, or verification command. An agent completing this work has no deterministic way to produce evidence that each AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.finding: The plan introduces a complex state machine (5 phases, FIFO selection, merge-vs-chain routing, N=5 re-drive loop, TOCTOU guard, fresh-Verified detection) but contains zero mention of a testing approach — no unit tests, integration tests, or acceptance test strategy for any of the specified behaviors, including multiple explicit failure paths (N=5 exhaustion → park, TOCTOU guard → re-drive, merge change never using /rebase:chain).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all items; Scope section

findings.finding: No proving command exists for any of the seven acceptance criteria — there is no test script, integration harness, or CLI invocation described that would produce concrete evidence of correctness for any claim.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all seven ACs

findings.finding: The plan specifies no testing approach for any of the new logic: the wipChain state machine, FIFO selection, rebase-routing branching (merge vs chain vs single), fresh-Verified SHA detection, TOCTOU guard, or the N=5 re-drive failure path that parks the change and surfaces `needs_rebase`. The N=5 exhaustion behavior is a new user-facing failure flow with zero test coverage mentioned.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Unit tests cover rebase-routing (merge/chain/single), fresh-SHA Verified detection (carried-vote rejection), and wipChain phase transitions. An integration test drives the N=5 re-drive exhaustion path end-to-end against a Gerrit test double and asserts the change is parked with `needs_rebase`.'

findings.finding: The plan contains zero proving commands or described tests for any of its seven acceptance criteria — no executor can verify done-ness without inventing the entire test strategy.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has an associated proving command or verification step — the plan defines six behavioral ACs but provides zero concrete commands (unit test invocation, integration test, API query, log assertion) that an executor or reviewer can run to confirm each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)

findings.finding: No testing plan exists for the substantial new state-machine logic introduced (wipChain phases, rebase-selection branching, fresh-Verified detection, TOCTOU guard, bounded re-drive loop). The plan contains zero mention of tests, test strategy, or test ACs, leaving the entire new behavior unvalidated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) and Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section to the plan with: (1) unit tests for the three-way rebase-selection rule using mocked RelatedChanges responses; (2) unit tests for fresh-Verified detection (carried-vote vs. new-patchset-vote); (3) state-machine tests cycling through all phase transitions; (4) a failure-path test for re-drive bound exhaustion (expect idle/error phase); (5) a TOCTOU test where main advances between submit-check and submit, verifying re-drive is triggered rather than stale submit.

findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new branching logic (rebase-selection rule, Verified-freshness check, TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering) but contains zero testing plan — no unit tests, integration tests, or deferred test child ticket — leaving all failure paths (SHA mismatch, stale vote, main advancing mid-flight, chain vs. single-change detection) completely unspecified for coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) remove the `needs_rebase` surfacing outcome from AC4 and defer it entirely to the failure-path story, or (b) keep it here and specify the exact surfacing mechanism (e.g., 'post a Gerrit inline comment on the front change with message X and remove the Autosubmit+1 vote') so the AC is implementable without the failure-path story.
findings.finding: AC4 includes a concrete failure-path outcome ('park the change and surface `needs_rebase`') while the Scope section explicitly defers 'Failure paths' to a separate story. This internal inconsistency means AC4 is either unimplementable (the surfacing mechanism is fully unspecified — no Gerrit comment, label, webhook, or log target is named) or it silently duplicates scope the failure-path story will re-specify, risking conflicting implementations.
findings.location: AC4 (FFO TOCTOU guard bullet); 'Scope' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Empirically verify `POST /a/changes/{id}/rebase:chain` with `{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}` against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (e.g. via a one-off curl against a throwaway change) and record the confirmed path + response shape in the plan or a referenced ADR before coding the loop.
findings.finding: The plan calls `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` (with `on_behalf_of_uploader=true`) but no existing repo code exercises this endpoint against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance. The colon-suffix REST path is a Gerrit-specific extension pattern introduced in Gerrit 3.6; while 3.14.1 should support it, neither the endpoint path nor the `on_behalf_of_uploader` JSON body key has been empirically probed against this instance. A silent 404 or 400 here would stall the entire loop.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-selection rule — `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` / chain variant; What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a spike/verification step: `curl -sS -X POST -u bot:token -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"on_behalf_of_uploader":true}' https://<host>/a/changes/<id>/rebase:chain` on a throwaway change and confirm HTTP 200 + the response `ChangeInfo` contains the new patchset. Record the confirmed path and parameter name in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `file_impact` list (or equivalent section) naming the concrete file paths for the new Python service — at minimum the module root (e.g. `infra/lander/__init__.py` or `scripts/lander/`), so edit-set accuracy and consumer blast-radius can be verified.
findings.finding: The plan names zero concrete file paths for the new Python service it creates. No module path (e.g. infra/lander/ or scripts/lander.py), no Dockerfile target, and no docker-compose service stanza is identified. This makes it impossible to (a) verify the artifact's location against the repo, (b) enumerate consumers/callers outside the artifact's directory, or (c) assess whether the new service correctly avoids touching src/rebar.
findings.location: What section / Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Files' or 'Edit targets' subsection to the plan listing the new module's home directory (e.g. `infra/lander/`) and any entry-point file. This mirrors how S1's ticket records `file_impact: [{path: infra/gerrit/project.config, ...}, {path: infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh, ...}]`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the minimum Gerrit permission set required for the bot account (specifically, name the exact Gerrit permission that enables `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` and confirm it is scoped to the target project/branch, not project-wide admin) in the plan's identity/attribution scope.
findings.finding: The plan exercises the `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` rebase parameter, meaning the bot credential must hold an 'Impersonate' or equivalent Gerrit permission that allows it to act as any uploader. This elevated capability is not declared as a scoped, minimum-necessary grant — the plan does not state which Gerrit permission set the bot account requires, nor does it constrain the impersonation to the specific change being landed.
findings.location: AC2 — 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the bot credential's secret-management contract (e.g., injected via env var from a vault/secrets manager, never stored in plaintext in code, config, or logs) — the plan's 'identity/attribution' scope section should name the mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a bot service identity that makes authenticated Gerrit REST API calls (rebase, submit, read votes) across a network boundary but declares no secret-management contract for the bot credential — no mention of whether it is injected via environment variable, vault, secrets manager, or config file, and no prohibition on plaintext storage.
findings.location: Scope section ('Happy-path loop + serialization + identity/attribution') and AC rebase/submit calls throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-bullet under Scope: 'Bot Gerrit HTTP credential is sourced from the project secrets manager (e.g., Vault / GCP Secret Manager) at startup via environment variable; never stored in config files or committed to the repository; not logged at any verbosity level.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `GET /a/changes/?q=label:Autosubmit+1+is:submittable&o=DETAILED_LABELS` returns a non-null `date` field in the `Autosubmit` label's `ApprovalInfo` entries on the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (after S1 deploys the label), or name the alternative field/query used for FIFO ordering.
findings.finding: The plan requires FIFO ordering 'keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp' but does not identify the specific Gerrit REST query and response field that surfaces per-vote timestamps. The `DETAILED_LABELS` option returns `ApprovalInfo[]` which includes a `date` field — but the plan does not name this field, and existing repo code (account_vote() in feature-branch-e2e.sh) only extracts the integer `value`, not `date`. Whether `date` is populated for custom project labels like `Autosubmit` on this instance is UNVERIFIED.
findings.location: AC line 1: 'FIFO ordering keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in AC4 that the FFO-landability check applies to the chain-bottom (oldest non-merge ancestor): 'confirm that the bottom-most ancestor's parent is still main's current tip' — and update tested_sha semantics or add a separate bottom_sha field that anchors this check.
findings.finding: The FFO-landability pre-submit check is described as confirming 'the tested SHA is still FFO-landable — its parent is still main's current tip.' For a relation chain, only the BOTTOM-MOST ancestor's parent must equal main's current tip; the tip change's parent is its predecessor in the chain, not main. The plan's language, combined with the single `tested_sha` field, implies the check is applied to one SHA (implicitly the tip), which would pass even when the chain-bottom has been displaced from main by another landing.
findings.location: AC4 ('FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `bottom_sha` (or `anchor_sha`) field to the wipChain schema representing the bottom-most ancestor's post-rebase SHA. The FFO pre-submit check becomes: `GET /changes/{bottom_id}` → `current_revision == bottom_sha AND parent == GET /projects/rebar/branches/main → revision`. Re-drive if either fails.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan (and in the S1/setup-project.sh provisioning) which Gerrit group the autolander-bot service account must join to hold the `Submit` ACL (currently `Contributors` or `Administrators`), and add it to the S2 or S1 acceptance criteria.
findings.finding: The plan does not state the auth preconditions for the autolander-bot service account: it must hold the `Submit` ACL to call `POST /changes/{id}/submit`, but `project.config` restricts `submit` to `group Contributors` + `group Administrators` with `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`. The plan neither names the bot's Gerrit account identity nor states that it will be added to `Contributors` (or `Administrators`). Without this, every `POST /submit` call returns 403.
findings.location: What section ('ancestor-atomic submit'); AC line 4 ('ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA'); infra/gerrit/project.config lines 62-64
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or a note: 'The autolander-bot Gerrit service account must be a member of `Contributors` (or `Administrators`) to satisfy the `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit` ACL in `project.config`; `setup-project.sh` (or its S2 analogue) must add it to that group on provisioning.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'ancestor-atomic submit' as a specific sequence of Gerrit REST calls (e.g. 'GET /changes/{id}?o=CURRENT_REVISION to confirm SHA == tested_sha, then immediately POST /changes/{id}/submit; if SHA mismatch, re-drive') so the atomicity property is implementable.
findings.finding: 'Ancestor-atomic submit' is used as a term of art but the concrete Gerrit REST call and any atomicity guarantee are never specified — an executor cannot know whether this is a plain POST /submit, a POST /submit with an expected-SHA guard, or something else.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 heading and What section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit auth precondition to the plan stating the credential type (HTTP Basic Auth over HTTPS `/a/` endpoints), credential source (e.g. SSM SecureString, same pattern as the review-bot in service-user.sh), and that a dedicated autolander-bot Gerrit service account must be provisioned before the loop can make mutating REST calls.
findings.finding: The plan does not state any auth/HTTPS preconditions for the loop's REST calls — it names no credential type (HTTP Basic Auth, bearer token, cookie), no secret storage mechanism (SSM, env var, mounted secret), and no provisioning step. The existing service-user.sh pattern (SSH-provisioned HTTP password → SSM → runtime env) is VERIFIED for the review-bot, but the plan does not reference or extend it for this new service.
findings.location: What section and Dependencies section — no auth preconditions stated anywhere

findings.finding: The rebase-selection rule in AC2 has no defined branch for a hybrid relation chain — non-merge ancestor changes beneath a `--no-ff` merge change at the chain tip — leaving an implementer with contradictory instructions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule AC (AC2)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify at least one observable signal per failure exit: structured log lines (with change_id and phase) for rebase failure, submit failure, and N=5 park; and define how `needs_rebase` is surfaced to the change owner (e.g. Gerrit comment, metric increment).
findings.finding: Beyond the S5 liveness heartbeat, the plan specifies no observability (metrics, structured logs, or alerts) for failure states: rebase failures, submit failures, N=5 re-drive exhaustion (`needs_rebase` park), or transient REST errors. Operators have no way to distinguish 'idle (no candidates)' from 'stuck on an error'.
findings.location: Scope section; AC: ancestor-atomic submit / re-drive / park

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the default Gerrit API field for the Autosubmit vote timestamp (e.g. 'the `grantedOn` epoch-second from the `Autosubmit` entry in the detailed label response at GET /changes/?q=label:Autosubmit+1+is:submittable&o=DETAILED_LABELS') so the executor uses the correct source.
findings.finding: The FIFO ordering key 'Autosubmit vote timestamp' is not grounded in a specific Gerrit API field, leaving the executor to guess the retrieval mechanism.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC1: 'Retrieve via `o=DETAILED_LABELS`; sort ascending on `labels.Autosubmit.all[].grantedOn`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the restart default for in-memory wipChain (e.g. 'on process start, wipChain is always initialized to idle/empty; any in-flight rebase from a prior run is re-detected and re-driven from the selecting phase on the next poll iteration').
findings.finding: The in-memory wipChain's restart behavior is unspecified — an executor cannot know whether process restart should attempt to recover in-flight state or reset to idle and re-select.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (wipChain schema) and What section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'On service start, wipChain initializes to {phase: idle}; no crash-recovery of prior wipChain state is attempted.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify heartbeat-write ownership: either add an AC to S2 ('The loop writes a liveness heartbeat record on each poll iteration') or explicitly state in the body that the heartbeat write is implemented in S5 and this story only documents the timing contract.
findings.finding: The plan body describes the bot writing a liveness heartbeat once per poll iteration as in-scope work for this service ('The bot writes its liveness heartbeat once per poll iteration (S5 AC5)'), but no AC in this plan verifies that behavior. The parenthetical '(S5 AC5)' is ambiguous: it could mean S5 defines and owns the criterion (so this plan need not cover it) or merely that S5 references it — leaving the heartbeat write as uncovered work in S2's implementation with no verifying criterion in either story until S5 ships.
findings.location: Poll cadence section ('The bot writes its liveness heartbeat once per poll iteration (S5 AC5)')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the error-handling contract for transient Gerrit REST errors (at minimum: retry with exponential backoff up to K attempts, then park the wipChain with an error phase and emit a log/metric).
findings.finding: The plan specifies no retry or backoff strategy for transient Gerrit REST errors (HTTP 429, 503, network blip) outside the TOCTOU re-drive path. A single transient failure on `POST /rebase`, `GET /related`, or the initial poll query has no defined recovery path.
findings.location: AC: rebase-selection rule; AC: ancestor-atomic submit / FFO TOCTOU guard

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the restart/recovery behavior for each wipChain phase, especially `submitting`: specify how the bot detects whether a submit succeeded after a crash (e.g. re-query change status on startup) and what recovery action it takes.
findings.finding: The wipChain is held in-memory only. A crash or restart during the `submitting` phase leaves ambiguous state: the `POST /submit` may have been accepted by Gerrit but not yet reflected in the bot's state. On restart the bot re-queries `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable`, but if the change was already submitted (and the label cleared) it will simply not be selected again — however, if the submit failed mid-flight, the change may remain in an inconsistent state with no recovery path documented.
findings.location: AC: wipChain schema (`phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle}`)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a startup reconciliation step: on bot start, query Gerrit for any change that has `Autosubmit+1` and was recently modified; if a change is in `MERGED` state, clear and move to idle; if still `NEW`, re-enter at `rebasing` phase.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define crash-recovery semantics for wipChain: specify either (a) a lightweight persistent store (e.g., a single JSON file or DB row) written before each phase transition so the loop can reconstruct state on restart, or (b) a Gerrit-query-based reconciliation step at startup that reconstructs phase from live Gerrit state (e.g., detect an already-rebased patchset with no Verified vote → re-enter `awaiting_verified` without another rebase).
findings.finding: The wipChain is purely in-memory with no crash-recovery or persistence specification. If the service restarts mid-flight (e.g., while in `awaiting_verified` phase after a rebase), the in-memory state is lost. The plan provides no mechanism to detect or recover from this: a re-started instance has no way to know a rebase already occurred, may re-select the same change, and will attempt a redundant rebase — potentially creating a new patchset and invalidating a Verified vote that was in-flight.
findings.location: AC — wipChain schema bullet; 'Scope' section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation mechanism — e.g. a dogfooding criterion ('a test agent session exercises the full land path end-to-end with no manual rebase step') or an operational metric target (e.g. 'zero `not fast-forward` submit errors per landing session in smoke test') — so the stated improvement is verifiably observable.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism (before/after workflow comparison, operational metric target, or dogfooding criterion) is present in any AC; all ACs are system-internal behavioral specs with no observable improvement anchor for the named stakeholder.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullet points)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Smoke-test: a scripted agent session creates a submittable change behind tip, sets Autosubmit+1, and the bot lands it without any manual rebase intervention within 2 poll cycles; observed in a staging Gerrit instance.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the lander may import `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.GerritClient` (extending it with rebase/submit/related-changes methods) rather than rebuilding Gerrit HTTP primitives in infra/; if the `project infra — NOT src/rebar core` boundary is intentional, document why the existing client cannot be reused or extracted to a shared location.
findings.finding: The plan explicitly places the lander in 'project infra — NOT src/rebar core', which means it cannot import the existing GerritClient (src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py). That client already implements the exact primitives the lander needs: authenticated urllib-based _request(), XSSI stripping, GerritError, basic-auth construction, and GET/POST wrappers. Implementing these again in infra/ is NIH duplication of live production code.
findings.location: ## What (first paragraph) and AC rebase-selection rule / FFO TOCTOU guard
findings.suggested_fix: Move the new Gerrit REST methods (rebase, submit, get_related_changes, get_branch_tip) into the existing GerritClient as additional methods, and either (a) allow the lander service to import from src/rebar/review_bot or (b) extract GerritClient to a standalone infra-consumable module. This is not an abstraction — it is direct extension of an existing class at an existing call-site cluster.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` accepts `on_behalf_of_uploader` as a request body field in Gerrit **3.14.1** (not just later releases) and that `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` is the correct endpoint path for chain rebase in 3.14.1 — cite the API doc or a live probe result in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO trailers (AC2), and that `POST /rebase:chain` is the correct chain-rebase endpoint — but no repo code exercises these Gerrit 3.14.1 REST endpoints and no documentation in the repo verifies that `on_behalf_of_uploader` is a supported request body field in the version deployed (gerritcodereview/gerrit:3.14.1). A prior plan-review cache entry for the parent epic (f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af) also flagged this as unverified. If `on_behalf_of_uploader` is not supported or has different semantics in 3.14.1, the DCO/Signed-off-by preservation guarantee silently fails and every bot-driven rebase strips authorship attribution.
findings.location: AC2 — Rebase-selection rule / `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` claim
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `verify_commands` entry or a footnote citing the Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API docs section for `RebaseInput` (e.g. `GET https://<host>/Documentation/rest-api-changes.html#rebase-change`) and confirm `on_behalf_of_uploader` is present in `RebaseInput` for 3.14.1 before treating AC2's authorship-preservation claim as satisfied.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove the S4 30 s poll cross-reference from the poll-cadence rationale (the 15 s choice stands on its own API-gentleness argument) or add S4 to the Dependencies list and specify its 30 s poll as a design constraint S4 must honour.
findings.finding: The poll-cadence rationale states the 15 s interval is 'intentionally distinct from `land`'s own 30 s client-side poll (S4)' — asserting S4 has a specific 30 s poll cadence as a fixed, known fact. S4 is a future downstream story (the plan scopes 'the agent-facing command' to a separate story); no S4 ticket, code, or spec exists in the repo that establishes this 30 s figure. The claim treats a not-yet-written story's design as an empirical fact.
findings.location: Poll cadence (design-fixed) section — 'distinct from land's own 30 s client-side poll (S4)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add S5 to the Dependencies list (so the AC5 heartbeat contract is a reviewable prerequisite) or inline the heartbeat write requirement directly into S2's own ACs rather than delegating it to a forward-reference.
findings.finding: The poll-cadence section states 'The bot writes its liveness heartbeat once per poll iteration (S5 AC5)' — asserting that S5 has a specific AC numbered AC5 governing heartbeat write behavior. S5 is a future story not listed in the Dependencies section; no S5 ticket, file, or artifact exists in the repo, and no AC5 text can be probed. Referencing a specific AC of an unwritten downstream story as a behavioral constraint is an unverifiable assertion.
findings.location: Poll cadence (design-fixed) section — 'S5 AC5' heartbeat reference

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify `on_behalf_of_uploader` against Gerrit 3.14.1's `RebaseInput` schema, confirm the required `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` capability, and add the ACL grant for the auto-lander bot service account to `infra/gerrit/project.config` and/or `service-user.sh`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` as a rebase parameter that preserves author identity, DCO `Signed-off-by`, and `rebar-ticket` trailers. This parameter name is unverified against Gerrit 3.14.1's `RebaseInput` schema. The actual Gerrit `RebaseInput` field is `on_behalf_of_uploader` (bool), introduced in Gerrit 3.5+, but it requires the rebasing account to hold the `rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader` capability or a specific ACL. Neither this capability nor its ACL grant are mentioned anywhere in the plan, the S1 config, or the existing `project.config`. If the bot's service account lacks this permission, the rebase silently runs under the bot's identity — minting patchsets with the bot as committer, stripping the original author's DCO trailers.
findings.location: AC — Rebase-selection rule: '`on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC5 as an observable behavioral outcome (e.g. 'the service exposes a /status endpoint or log line that emits the current wipChain phase and tested_sha on each poll iteration, observable in CI output') rather than an implementation schema description.
findings.finding: AC5 ('wipChain schema') is effort-framed ('a single in-memory record … held by the single instance') rather than stating an observable outcome for the user/system, making it non-measurable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or Scope item) to produce a lightweight ADR or design-doc section capturing: the auto-lander service's role, the REST-submittable vs FFO-landable semantic rule, the wipChain schema, the TOCTOU guard algorithm, and the 15 s poll-cadence as a cross-story contract referenced by S4/S5.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural service and several novel contracts (wipChain schema, REST-submittable vs FFO-landable rule, TOCTOU guard, 15 s poll-cadence that S4 and S5 depend on) but names no ADR or design-doc artifact that would capture these decisions for future maintainers and cross-story consumers.
findings.location: ## What / ## Dependencies / AC (wipChain schema bullet)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope bullet: 'Create ADR-XXXX (auto-lander-service contract) documenting: service role, wipChain schema, FFO-landable vs REST-submittable distinction, TOCTOU guard, 15 s poll cadence (shared with S4/S5), N=5 re-drive cap.' Reference it from the S4 and S5 dependency lines.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a spike/probe plan (or a RED fixture) to empirically derive the 15 s poll cadence (measure Gerrit API rate-limit headroom under expected concurrency) and the N=5 re-drive cap (simulate or instrument how frequently main advances during a rebase-await-verified cycle) before hardcoding them.
findings.finding: The 15 s poll interval is declared 'design-fixed' and the N=5 re-drive cap is stated without derivation from data or any plan to validate them empirically; both are asserted thresholds whose correctness cannot be established by reasoning alone.
findings.location: ## What — Poll cadence (design-fixed) / AC TOCTOU guard bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Make the re-drive cap (`N=5`) a named configuration parameter (e.g. env-var `AUTOLANDER_MAX_REDRIVES`, defaulting to 5) so it can be tuned per environment without a code change.
findings.finding: The N=5 re-drive limit is a business rule threshold hardcoded in the AC with no mention of making it configurable, yet it is the kind of operationally-tunable value (depends on main's commit velocity) that is expected to evolve.
findings.location: AC — TOCTOU guard + re-drive bullet: 'looping at most N=5 times'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'The re-drive cap N is read from config (env-var `AUTOLANDER_MAX_REDRIVES`, default 5); the poll interval is read from config (env-var `AUTOLANDER_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS`, default 15) even if treated as design-fixed for now.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a per-call HTTP timeout (e.g. 30 s) for every Gerrit REST call in the poll loop, and define what the loop does on timeout (log + retry next iteration vs. immediate retry).
findings.finding: No per-call HTTP timeout is specified for any Gerrit REST call in the poll loop. A stalled call (e.g. on `POST /rebase:chain`, `POST /submit`, or the poll query) would block the loop indefinitely, halting all landing activity with no operator signal.
findings.location: What / Poll cadence section; AC: rebase-selection rule, ancestor-atomic submit
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'All Gerrit REST calls are made with a per-call timeout of T seconds (suggested: 30 s); a timeout is treated as a transient error and retried on the next poll iteration after exponential backoff.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to the plan that the deploy story must include a feature flag or dry-run mode for initial activation, and specify the expected coexistence behavior between the bot and existing agent-driven `land` commands during rollout.
findings.finding: The plan defers deploy to a separate story but does not note that this new always-on bot directly affects `main`-branch submissions and will need a staged rollout or feature flag. Without that note, the deploy story may not know to plan for controlled activation.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Failure paths, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a design note: 'The bot SHOULD support a `--dry-run` flag that logs intended actions without calling `POST /rebase` or `POST /submit`, enabling safe shadow-mode validation before full activation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the serialization mechanism explicitly: name the concurrency model (e.g., 'single asyncio event loop, no threads'), whether the service must run as exactly one replica, and whether an external lock (e.g., a Gerrit 'wip' label toggle or a process file-lock) guards against multi-instance races.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'serialized' and 'single instance / never rebases multiple changes concurrently' but does not specify the serialization MECHANISM. An implementer has no guidance on whether to use a threading lock, a GIL-reliant single-threaded asyncio loop, a process-level file lock, or a distributed mutex — each has materially different correctness and deployment properties.
findings.location: AC — 'Single wipChain / single instance (serialized)' bullet

findings.finding: The plan's Dependencies section characterizes S1's output as an 'autolander-bot vote ACL', but S1's ticket (843b-801f-9ef9-442d) defines the Autosubmit vote ACL as granted to 'Contributors' (requesters, not bot-only): `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`. This is an empirically-checkable assertion about a named dependency's output that the ticket probe directly refutes — the ACL is not bot-restricted.
findings.location: Dependencies section: 'the `Autosubmit` label + `autolander-bot` vote ACL'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the `Autosubmit` label + `autolander-bot` vote ACL' to 'the `Autosubmit` label (contributor-votable, `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`) + the bot's ability to read that label'; remove any implicit assumption that only the bot can set Autosubmit+1.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
